option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/Mikhail Senatorov/Documents/GitHub/AndroidStudioProjects/Breathe":"{\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/BreatheScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function `BreatheScreen` that represents the main screen of a breathing exercise application. The file also includes several helper functions and constants used within the `BreatheScreen` function.\\n\\n`BreatheScreen`: Composable function that renders the main screen of the breathing exercise application, including the breathing circle, phase instructions, elapsed time, and completion overlay.\\n\\n`formatElapsed`: Helper function that formats the elapsed time in seconds as a string in the format \\\"mm:ss\\\".\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/theme/Theme.kt\":\"This file defines the main app theme for the \\\"Breathe\\\" application, which is a dark-only theme. It provides a `BreatheTheme` function that sets up the Material3 color scheme and typography based on a custom `AppColors` palette.\\n\\n`AppColors`: A custom color palette for the app\\u0027s theme.\\n\\n`BreatheTheme`: Sets up the Material3 color scheme and typography based on the provided `AppColors` palette.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/worker/SyncWorker.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`SyncWorker`: A worker class that runs when the device has network connectivity and pushes any unsynchronized sessions from the local database to the backend using the `SessionRepository`.\\n\\n`SyncWorker.doWork()`: The main work function of the `SyncWorker` class, which attempts to synchronize the unsynchronized sessions and handles retries in case of exceptions.\\n\\n`SyncWorker.schedule()`: A static function that enqueues a one-time `SyncWorker` task to run as soon as the device has network connectivity, using an exponential backoff policy.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for implementing a background worker that synchronizes unsynchronized session data from the local database to the backend, ensuring that the data is reliably pushed to the server.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/models/ApiModels.kt\":\"This file contains data classes that represent the models used in the Breathe app. It includes classes for authentication, user profiles, sessions, challenges, and integrations. Here\\u0027s a summary of each class and function:\\n\\n`LoginRequest`: A data class that represents the request body for a login operation.\\n`RegisterRequest`: A data class that represents the request body for a registration operation.\\n`AuthResponse`: A data class that represents the response from an authentication operation, containing a token and user information.\\n`User`: A data class that represents a user\\u0027s profile information.\\n`BodyProfile`: A data class that represents a user\\u0027s body profile information.\\n`UpdateProfileRequest`: A data class that represents the request body for updating a user\\u0027s profile.\\n`RemoteSession`: A data class that represents a user\\u0027s session data.\\n`CreateSessionRequest`: A data class that represents the request body for creating a new session.\\n`BadgeDto`: A data class that represents a badge earned by a user.\\n`ChallengeDto`: A data class that represents a challenge.\\n`UserChallengeDto`: A data class that represents a user\\u0027s progress in a challenge.\\n`ChallengeRecommendationDto`: A data class that represents a recommended challenge for a user.\\n`CheckInResponse`: A data class that represents the response from a challenge check-in operation.\\n`IntegrationStatusDto`: A data class that represents the status of an integration.\\n`IntegrationDataDto`: A data class that represents the data from an integration.\\n`SleepDayDto`: A data class that represents a day\\u0027s sleep data.\\n`HrvDayDto`: A data class that represents a day\\u0027s heart rate variability data.\\n`HrDayDto`: A data class that represents a day\\u0027s heart rate data.\",\"/app/src/androidTest/java/com/example/breathe/ExampleInstrumentedTest.kt\":\"This file contains an instrumented test for an Android app called \\\"Breathe\\\". It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a single test case that checks if the app\\u0027s package name matches the expected value.\\n\\n`ExampleInstrumentedTest`: This is an instrumented test class that runs on an Android device and tests the app\\u0027s context.\\n\\n`useAppContext()`: This test function checks if the app\\u0027s package name matches the expected value, which is \\\"com.example.breathe\\\".\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/api/ApiService.kt\":\"This file defines an `ApiService` interface that provides methods for interacting with a backend API. The file also includes a `GoogleLoginRequest` data class.\\n\\n`ApiService`: Defines the API endpoints and methods for authentication, user profile management, session management, and challenge management.\\n\\n`updateProfile`: Updates the user\\u0027s profile.\\n`login`: Authenticates the user and returns an authentication response.\\n`register`: Registers a new user and returns an authentication response.\\n`loginWithGoogle`: Authenticates the user using a Google access token and returns an authentication response.\\n`getProfile`: Retrieves the user\\u0027s profile.\\n`getSessions`: Retrieves the user\\u0027s sessions.\\n`createSession`: Creates a new session.\\n`getChallenges`: Retrieves a list of all challenges.\\n`getMyChallenges`: Retrieves the user\\u0027s challenges.\\n`getChallengeRecommendation`: Retrieves a challenge recommendation.\\n`joinChallenge`: Joins a challenge.\\n`checkIn`: Checks in to a challenge.\\n`abandonChallenge`: Abandons a challenge.\\n\\n`GoogleLoginRequest`: A data class that holds a Google access token for use in the `loginWithGoogle` function.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/StatsScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Composable function `StatsScreen` that displays various statistics and insights related to the user\\u0027s meditation activity. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`StatsScreen`: Composable function that displays the statistics and insights screen.\\n`PeriodTabs`: Composable function that displays the period selection tabs (week, month, year).\\n`ActivityChart`: Composable function that displays the activity chart based on the selected period.\\n`MilestoneBadges`: Composable function that displays the milestone badges (streak, total time, sessions, average calmness).\\n`BadgeCard`: Composable function that displays a single milestone badge.\\n`MoodGrid`: Composable function that displays the mood grid, showing the changes in mood over time.\\n`MoodCell`: Composable function that displays a single mood cell in the mood grid.\\n`LegendDot`: Composable function that displays a legend dot for the mood grid.\\n`InsightCardRow`: Composable function that displays a single AI insight card.\\n`QuickLinkRow`: Composable function that displays a quick link row.\\n`SectionLabel`: Composable function that displays a section label.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/repository/SleepRepository.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`SleepRepository`: This class is responsible for managing the data related to the user\\u0027s sleep patterns and history.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It appears to be a placeholder for a repository class that will handle the data storage and retrieval for the user\\u0027s sleep-related information.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/HistoryScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Compose-based UI screen for displaying the history of breathing sessions. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`HistoryScreen`: A composable function that renders the history screen, including a header, content area, and handling of different states (loading, empty, error, success).\\n\\n`SessionHistoryRow`: A composable function that renders a single row in the history list, displaying the session type, date, and duration.\\n\\n`ShimmerRow`: A composable function that renders a shimmer effect for a loading state row.\\n\\n`typeIcon`: A function that returns the appropriate icon for a given session type.\\n\\n`typeLabel`: A function that returns the appropriate label for a given session type.\\n\\n`fmtMin`: A function that formats the session duration in minutes.\\n\\n`formatDateTime`: A function that formats the session completion date and time.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for rendering the history screen, which displays a list of past breathing sessions, with appropriate handling for different states (loading, empty, error, success).\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/MainActivity.kt\":\"This file is the main entry point for the Android application \\\"Breathe\\\". It sets up the application\\u0027s UI, navigation, and theme management. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`MainActivity`: The main activity of the application that sets up the UI and navigation.\\n\\n`dataStore`: A Kotlin extension property that provides access to the application\\u0027s data store for storing and retrieving user preferences.\\n\\n`onCreate`: The main entry point of the `MainActivity` class that initializes the application\\u0027s UI and sets up the navigation and theme management.\\n\\n`BreatheTheme`: A Compose function that sets the application\\u0027s theme based on the user\\u0027s preference.\\n\\n`AppNavGraph`: A Compose function that sets up the application\\u0027s navigation graph and handles theme changes.\\n\\n`onThemeChange`: A callback function passed to `AppNavGraph` that updates the user\\u0027s theme preference in the data store.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/StatsViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a ViewModel class and several supporting data classes for a meditation app. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions:\\n\\n`DayMinutes`: A data class representing the minutes meditated on a specific date.\\n`MoodPoint`: A data class representing the change in mood before and after a meditation session.\\n`InsightCard`: A data class representing an AI-generated insight about the user\\u0027s meditation practice.\\n`StatsState`: A data class representing the state of the meditation statistics screen.\\n`StatsViewModel`: A ViewModel class that manages the state and data for the meditation statistics screen, including fetching data from a local database and a remote API, and computing various statistics and insights.\\n\\nThe file also contains several helper functions for converting between date formats, computing streaks, building chart data, and formatting meditation time.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/models/SleepData.kt\":\"This file contains a single data class:\\n\\n`SleepData`: A data class that represents a sleep session, including the start and end times.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a data class to represent sleep data, including the start and end times of a sleep session.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/navigation/MainScreen.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the main breathing exercise screen in the Breathe app. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`BreathingPattern`: A data class that represents a breathing pattern with inhale, hold, exhale, and hold after exhale durations.\\n\\n`TechniqueInfo`: A data class that holds information about a specific breathing technique, including its type, emoji, name, pattern, and description.\\n\\n`Phase`: An enum that represents the different phases of a breathing cycle (inhale, hold1, exhale, hold2).\\n\\n`MainScreen`: The main composable function that renders the breathing exercise screen, including the breathing animation, duration and pattern selection, and the list of available breathing techniques.\\n\\n`SettingItem`: A composable function that renders a setting item with a title, value, and clickable functionality.\\n\\n`TechniqueRow`: A composable function that renders a row for a specific breathing technique, including its emoji, name, pattern, and description.\\n\\n`SettingsDialog`, `ThemeDialog`, `DurationDialog`, and `PatternDialog`: Composable functions that render the respective dialogs for the app\\u0027s settings, theme selection, duration selection, and breathing pattern selection.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/HistoryViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a ViewModel class and several supporting data classes and functions for a meditation app.\\n\\n`HistoryState`: A data class that represents the state of the meditation session history.\\n\\n`HistoryViewModel`: A ViewModel class that manages the state of the meditation session history, including fetching data from an API and displaying it in the UI.\\n\\n`observeLocal()`: A function that collects the local meditation sessions from a repository and updates the state accordingly.\\n\\n`syncFromApi()`: A function that fetches the meditation sessions from an API and updates the state accordingly.\\n\\n`fetch()`: A function that triggers the fetching of meditation sessions from the API, updating the state with a loading status if the list is currently empty.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides the logic for managing the meditation session history in the app, including fetching data from both local and remote sources and updating the UI accordingly.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/local/AppDatabase.kt\":\"This file defines the Room database for the Breathe app, which is used to store meditation session data.\\n\\n`AppDatabase`: A Room database class that defines the schema and migrations for the meditation session data.\\n\\n`MIGRATION_1_2`: A migration that adds a new column `isSynced` to the `meditation_sessions` table.\\n\\n`MIGRATION_2_3`: A migration that adds two new columns, `remoteId` and `type`, to the `meditation_sessions` table.\",\"/settings.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle configuration file for an Android project named \\\"Breathe\\\". It sets up the plugin management and dependency resolution for the project, specifying the repositories to be used and the rules for resolving dependencies. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather configures the build environment for the project.\\n\\n`pluginManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving plugin dependencies, including Google, Maven Central, and the Gradle Plugin Portal.\\n\\n`dependencyResolutionManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving project dependencies, including Google and Maven Central, and sets the repositories mode to fail on project repositories.\\n\\n`rootProject.name`: Sets the name of the root project to \\\"Breathe\\\".\\n\\n`include(\\\":app\\\")`: Includes the \\\":app\\\" module in the project.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/theme/Type.kt\":\"This file defines the typography styles for the Breathe app using Jetpack Compose. It includes the following:\\n\\n`MontserratFontFamily`: A custom font family that includes various weights and styles of the Montserrat font, which is bundled locally with the app.\\n\\n`AppTypography`: A `Typography` object that defines the text styles for different UI elements in the app, such as display, headlines, titles, body, and labels.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up the typography system for the Breathe app, ensuring a consistent and visually appealing user interface.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/AuthViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a ViewModel class for handling authentication-related logic in an Android application.\\n\\n`AuthUiState`: A sealed interface that represents the different states of the authentication UI, such as Idle, Loading, Success, and Error.\\n\\n`AuthViewModel`: A ViewModel class that manages the state of the authentication process, including login, registration, and Google sign-in. It interacts with an `AuthRepository` to perform these actions and updates the UI state accordingly.\\n\\n`login(email: String, password: String)`: Initiates a login process with the provided email and password, updating the `loginState` accordingly.\\n\\n`register(name: String, email: String, password: String)`: Initiates a registration process with the provided name, email, and password, updating the `registerState` accordingly.\\n\\n`loginWithGoogle(idToken: String)`: Initiates a Google sign-in process with the provided ID token, updating the `loginState` accordingly.\\n\\n`resetLoginState()`: Resets the `loginState` to the Idle state.\\n\\n`resetRegisterState()`: Resets the `registerState` to the Idle state.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/HomeScreen.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `HomeScreen` composable function, which is the main screen of the Breathe app. It displays various UI elements, such as a greeting, today\\u0027s stats, a featured \\\"Start Breathing\\\" card, quick exercise cards, and recent sessions. The file also includes several helper composables and pure helper functions to support the main screen.\\n\\n`Exercise`: A data class representing an exercise with a route type, title, subtitle, and description.\\n\\n`QuickExercise`: A data class representing a quick exercise with a route type, label, duration, and icon.\\n\\n`HomeScreen`: The main composable function that renders the home screen of the Breathe app.\\n\\n`StatCard`: A composable function that renders a card displaying a statistic with a label, value, and unit.\\n\\n`FeaturedCard`: A composable function that renders a featured \\\"Start Breathing\\\" card with a gradient background and a decorative glow ring.\\n\\n`QuickExerciseCard`: A composable function that renders a card for a quick exercise with an icon, label, and duration.\\n\\n`SessionRow`: A composable function that renders a row for a recent session with the session type, duration, and completion time.\\n\\n`EmptySessionsState`: A composable function that renders a state when there are no recent sessions.\\n\\n`ErrorSessionsState`: A composable function that renders an error state when there is an issue fetching recent sessions.\\n\\n`typeLabel`: A pure helper function that returns a label for a session type.\\n\\n`fmtDuration`: A pure helper function that formats the duration of a session.\\n\\n`relativeTime`: A pure helper function that returns a relative time string for a session completion time.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/SplashScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Kotlin Compose-based UI screen for the \\\"Breathe\\\" app\\u0027s splash screen.\\n\\n`SplashViewModel`: A ViewModel class that checks if the user is logged in by using a `TokenManager`.\\n\\n`SplashScreen`: The main composable function that displays the app\\u0027s logo with an animated fade-in and scale effect, and then navigates to either the login or home screen based on the user\\u0027s login status.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/repository/ChallengesRepository.kt\":\"This file is a Kotlin class called `ChallengesRepository` that serves as a repository for managing challenges in a mobile application. It interacts with an API service to perform various operations related to challenges, such as retrieving a list of available challenges, getting the user\\u0027s active challenges, getting a challenge recommendation, joining a challenge, checking in to a challenge, and abandoning a challenge.\\n\\n`ChallengesRepository`: A repository class that manages challenges in a mobile application.\\n\\n`getChallenges()`: Retrieves a list of all available challenges.\\n`getMyChallenges()`: Retrieves the user\\u0027s active challenges.\\n`getChallengeRecommendation()`: Retrieves an AI-generated challenge recommendation.\\n`joinChallenge(slug: String)`: Allows the user to join a challenge.\\n`checkIn(challengeId: String)`: Allows the user to check in to a challenge.\\n`abandonChallenge(challengeId: String)`: Allows the user to abandon a challenge.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/utils/AuthEventBus.kt\":\"This file defines an `AuthEvent` sealed class and an `AuthEventBus` object that serves as a process-wide event bus for authentication events.\\n\\n`AuthEvent`: A sealed class that represents authentication events, with a single `Unauthorized` data object.\\n\\n`AuthEventBus`: A Kotlin object that provides a non-suspending, thread-safe way to emit and observe authentication events using a `SharedFlow`. It is designed to be used from OkHttp interceptors, which run on a thread pool outside of any coroutine or DI context.\\n\\n`emit`: A function that allows non-suspending emission of `AuthEvent` instances to the `AuthEventBus`.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/repository/AuthRepository.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `AuthRepository`, which is responsible for handling authentication-related operations in the application.\\n\\n`AuthRepository`: A repository class that provides methods for user authentication, including login, registration, and Google login, as well as logout functionality.\\n\\n`login(email: String, password: String)`: Logs in a user with the provided email and password, and saves the authentication token.\\n\\n`register(name: String, email: String, password: String)`: Registers a new user with the provided name, email, and password, and saves the authentication token.\\n\\n`loginWithGoogle(idToken: String)`: Logs in a user using their Google account, and saves the authentication token.\\n\\n`logout()`: Clears the saved authentication token.\\n\\n`httpError(code: Int, rawBody: String?)`: A helper function that provides a human-readable error message based on the HTTP response code.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/components/AuthComponents.kt\":\"This file contains two Composable functions and one helper function:\\n\\n`AuthTextField`: A styled `OutlinedTextField` for use in authentication screens, with a focus on readability on dark backgrounds.\\n\\n`PasswordTextField`: An `OutlinedTextField` with an integrated show/hide toggle for password fields.\\n\\n`authFieldColors`: A helper function that provides a shared color scheme for all authentication-related input fields.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide custom, styled text input components for authentication screens in a Compose-based Android application, with a focus on consistent appearance and readability.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/di/DatabaseModule.kt\":\"This file is a Dagger Hilt module that provides dependencies for the Breathe app\\u0027s database and repository.\\n\\n`DatabaseModule`: A Dagger Hilt module that provides the necessary dependencies for the app\\u0027s database and repository.\\n\\n`provideDatabase`: Provides an instance of the `AppDatabase` class, which is the Room database for the app, and sets up the database with migrations and a callback.\\n\\n`provideMeditationDao`: Provides an instance of the `MeditationDao` interface, which is the data access object for the meditation table in the database.\\n\\n`provideMeditationRepository`: Provides an instance of the `MeditationRepository` class, which is the repository for the meditation data.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/utils/UserPrefs.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a shared `DataStore` delegate for user settings (notifications, reminder time, etc.) and a set of `Preferences` keys for accessing those settings. The purpose of this file is to provide a centralized location for managing user preferences that can be accessed by various components of the application, such as `BroadcastReceivers` and `ViewModels`, without creating duplicate instances of the `DataStore`.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/api/RetrofitClient.kt\":\"This file is a Hilt module that provides the necessary dependencies for the Retrofit network client and API service. It does not contain any classes, but rather provides the following functions:\\n\\n`provideOkHttpClient`: Configures the OkHttpClient with interceptors for attaching the authorization token, handling 401 Unauthorized responses, and logging request/response bodies in debug builds.\\n\\n`provideRetrofit`: Provides a Retrofit instance with the base URL and the configured OkHttpClient.\\n\\n`provideApiService`: Provides an instance of the `ApiService` interface, which is created using the Retrofit instance.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to set up the network-related dependencies for the application, including the OkHttpClient, Retrofit, and API service, using Hilt\\u0027s dependency injection framework.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/utils/TokenManager.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`TokenManager`: A singleton class that provides secure storage for a JWT token using EncryptedSharedPreferences, which uses AES-256 encryption.\\n\\n`saveToken(token: String)`: Saves the provided JWT token to the encrypted shared preferences.\\n\\n`getToken(): String?`: Retrieves the stored JWT token from the encrypted shared preferences.\\n\\n`clearToken()`: Removes the stored JWT token from the encrypted shared preferences.\\n\\n`isLoggedIn(): Boolean`: Checks if a JWT token is currently stored in the encrypted shared preferences, indicating the user is logged in.\\n\\n`buildEncryptedPrefs(): SharedPreferences`: Constructs the EncryptedSharedPreferences instance used to store the JWT token.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a secure way to store and retrieve a JWT token on the Android device, using the Android Jetpack Security library\\u0027s EncryptedSharedPreferences feature.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/receiver/BootReceiver.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and no functions.\\n\\n`BootReceiver`: This class is a `BroadcastReceiver` that listens for the `Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED` broadcast, which is triggered when the device boots up. If the user had notifications enabled before the device reboot, this class will reschedule the daily reminder alarm using the `NotificationScheduler` class.\\n\\nThe file as a whole is responsible for restoring the daily reminder alarm after a device reboot, as the `AlarmManager` alarms are cleared on reboot.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/components/SessionFeedbackModal.kt\":\"This file contains a Compose UI component for a session feedback modal. It includes several composable functions that handle different steps of the feedback process, such as mood, feelings, session quality, and notes. Here\\u0027s a list of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`SessionFeedbackModal`: A composable function that renders the session feedback modal, including the step-by-step feedback process.\\n\\n`StepHeader`: A composable function that renders the header for each step of the feedback process.\\n\\n`StepMood`: A composable function that renders the mood slider for before and after the session.\\n\\n`MoodSliderRow`: A composable function that renders a single mood slider row.\\n\\n`StepFeelings`: A composable function that renders the feelings selection UI.\\n\\n`StepQuality`: A composable function that renders the session quality ratings.\\n\\n`DotSliderRow`: A composable function that renders a slider row with dot indicators.\\n\\n`StepNotes`: A composable function that renders the notes input and summary.\\n\\n`SummaryChip`: A composable function that renders a summary chip.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/ProfileViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `ProfileViewModel`, which is a ViewModel class responsible for managing the state and logic related to the user\\u0027s profile in the Breathe app.\\n\\n`ProfileState`: A data class that represents the state of the user\\u0027s profile, including information such as username, email, avatar, height, weight, age, gender, goal, session statistics, and notification settings.\\n\\n`ProfileViewModel`: This ViewModel class is responsible for fetching the user\\u0027s profile from the remote API, observing and updating the local session statistics, managing the user\\u0027s notification settings, and handling the logout functionality.\\n\\nThe file also includes several private helper functions that are used within the `ProfileViewModel` class, such as `epochToDate()`, `computeCurrentStreak()`, and `computeLongestStreak()`.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/models/HeartRateData.kt\":\"This file contains a single data class:\\n\\n`HeartRateData`: A data class that represents a heart rate measurement, including an ID, timestamp, and beats per minute (BPM).\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a data class to represent heart rate data, which can be used in the application\\u0027s data models.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/theme/Color.kt\":\"This file defines the color palette and theme-related classes and functions for the Breathe app. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the following:\\n\\n`AppColors`: A data class that encapsulates the app-specific color roles, such as background, surface, primary, and various text colors.\\n\\n`DefaultDarkColors`: An instance of `AppColors` representing the default dark theme for the app.\\n\\n`OceanThemeColors`: An instance of `AppColors` representing the Ocean theme for the app.\\n\\n`ForestThemeColors`: An instance of `AppColors` representing the Forest theme for the app.\\n\\n`SunsetThemeColors`: An instance of `AppColors` representing the Sunset theme for the app.\\n\\n`LocalAppColors`: A `CompositionLocal` that provides the current theme\\u0027s `AppColors` instance to the app\\u0027s screens without the need to pass it as a parameter.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/receiver/ReminderReceiver.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`ReminderReceiver`: A BroadcastReceiver that is triggered when the daily alarm goes off, showing a notification and rescheduling the alarm for the next day.\\n\\n`showNotification`: A function that creates and displays a notification with a title, body, and a pending intent to open the MainActivity.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for handling the daily reminder notification in the Breathe app. When the alarm is triggered, it displays the notification and reschedules the alarm for the next day, ensuring the user is reminded to use the app daily.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/repository/MeditationRepository.kt\":\"This file contains a `MeditationRepository` class and several functions that interact with a `MeditationDao` to manage meditation session data.\\n\\n`MeditationRepository`: A repository class that provides an abstraction layer for interacting with the meditation session data.\\n\\n`getAllSessions()`: Retrieves a flow of all meditation sessions.\\n`getTotalsByDay()`: Retrieves a flow of daily meditation session totals.\\n`insertSession(session: MeditationSession)`: Inserts a new meditation session.\\n`markAsSynced(id: Long)`: Marks a meditation session as synchronized.\\n`updateRemoteId(id: Long, remoteId: String)`: Updates the remote ID of a meditation session.\\n`getUnsyncedSessions()`: Retrieves a list of meditation sessions that have not been synchronized.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/models/MeditationSession.kt\":\"This file defines a data class `MeditationSession` that represents a meditation session in the application\\u0027s database. The class has several properties, including the session duration, date, sync status, remote ID, and type. The file does not contain any functions, only the data class definition.\\n\\n`MeditationSession`: A data class that represents a meditation session, with properties for duration, date, sync status, remote ID, and type.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/HomeViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a ViewModel class for a home screen in an Android app. It fetches user profile information and meditation session data from an API and local database, and updates the UI state accordingly.\\n\\n`SessionsStatus`: An sealed interface representing the different states of the API sessions fetch.\\n\\n`HomeUiState`: A data class representing the UI state for the home screen.\\n\\n`HomeViewModel`: The ViewModel class that manages the home screen\\u0027s UI state.\\n\\n`fetchProfile()`: Fetches the user\\u0027s profile information from the API and updates the UI state.\\n\\n`fetchSessions()`: Fetches the user\\u0027s recent meditation sessions from the API and updates the UI state.\\n\\n`observeLocalSessions()`: Observes the user\\u0027s local meditation sessions, calculates the total minutes meditated today and the current streak, and updates the UI state.\\n\\n`epochToDate()`: Converts an epoch timestamp to a `LocalDate` object.\\n\\n`computeCurrentStreak()`: Calculates the user\\u0027s current meditation streak based on the dates of their completed sessions.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/ProfileScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Compose-based UI for a profile screen in an Android app. It includes various sections such as profile information, session history, progress, challenges, and device connections. The file also includes several helper functions and data classes to support the UI.\\n\\n`ProfileScreen`: Composable function that renders the main profile screen.\\n\\n`HeaderCard`: Composable function that renders the header card with the user\\u0027s profile information.\\n\\n`TabBar`: Composable function that renders the tab bar for navigating between different sections of the profile screen.\\n\\n`ProfileTab`: Composable function that renders the profile section of the profile screen.\\n\\n`SessionsTab`: Composable function that renders the sessions section of the profile screen.\\n\\n`ProgressTab`: Composable function that renders the progress section of the profile screen.\\n\\n`ChallengesTab`: Composable function that renders the challenges section of the profile screen.\\n\\n`DevicesTab`: Composable function that renders the devices section of the profile screen.\\n\\n`SectionCard`: Composable function that renders a section card with a title and content.\\n\\n`SmallStatCard`: Composable function that renders a small statistic card.\\n\\n`QuickNavCard`: Composable function that renders a quick navigation card.\\n\\n`TabButton`: Composable function that renders a tab button.\\n\\n`Chip`: Composable function that renders a chip.\\n\\n`FlowPills`: Composable function that renders a flow of pills.\\n\\n`MiniInput`: Composable function that renders a mini input field.\\n\\n`ReminderTimePickerDialog`: Composable function that renders a time picker dialog for setting a reminder.\\n\\n`initials`: Helper function that generates initials from a name.\\n\\n`computeBmi`: Helper function that computes the BMI from height and weight.\\n\\n`uriToBase64`: Helper function that converts a URI to a base64-encoded string.\\n\\n`base64ToBitmap`: Helper function that converts a base64-encoded string to a bitmap.\",\"/app/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script for an Android application. It sets up the project configuration, dependencies, and plugins required for the application. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather configures the build process for the Android application.\\n\\nThe key aspects of the build script are:\\n\\n1. Applying various plugins, such as the Android application plugin, Kotlin Android plugin, Compose plugin, Dagger Hilt plugin, and KSP (Kotlin Symbol Processing) plugin.\\n2. Configuring the Android application settings, including the namespace, compile SDK version, minimum SDK version, target SDK version, and version information.\\n3. Enabling Jetpack Compose and build config features.\\n4. Setting the Kotlin compiler options to use Java 11 as the target.\\n5. Declaring the dependencies for the application, including Jetpack Compose, Android core libraries, networking libraries (OkHttp, Retrofit), navigation, ViewModel, LiveData, Coroutines, Room, Hilt, WorkManager, Material Design, and various testing libraries.\\n6. Configuring the KSP plugin to enable incremental Room compilation.\\n\\nOverall, this build script sets up the necessary configuration and dependencies for an Android application that uses Jetpack Compose, Dagger Hilt, Room, and other modern Android libraries and frameworks.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/repository/SessionRepository.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`SessionRepository`: A repository class that serves as the single source of truth for meditation sessions, handling the synchronization between the local Room database and the remote API.\\n\\n`getAll()`: Retrieves all meditation sessions from the local Room database, ordered by the newest first, and returns a flow that reacts to database changes.\\n\\n`sync()`: Pushes all unsynchronized meditation sessions from the local Room database to the remote API, updating the session records with the remote IDs and marking them as synchronized.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for managing the data flow between the local database and the remote API for meditation sessions, ensuring that the data is consistently synchronized between the two sources.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/data/local/dao/MeditationDao.kt\":\"This file contains a data access object (DAO) interface for a Room database, which is used to interact with the `MeditationSession` data model.\\n\\n`DayTotal`: A data class that represents the total duration of meditation sessions for a given day.\\n\\n`MeditationDao`:\\n- `insertSession(session: MeditationSession)`: Inserts a new meditation session into the database and returns the auto-generated row ID.\\n- `markAsSynced(id: Long)`: Updates the `isSynced` flag of a meditation session to `true`.\\n- `updateRemoteId(id: Long, remoteId: String)`: Updates the `remoteId` and `isSynced` flag of a meditation session.\\n- `getUnsyncedSessions()`: Retrieves a list of all meditation sessions that have not been synced.\\n- `getAllSessions()`: Retrieves a flow of all meditation sessions, ordered by date in descending order.\\n- `getTotalsByDay()`: Retrieves a flow of `DayTotal` objects, which represent the total duration of meditation sessions for each day, ordered by day in descending order.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/LoginScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Composable function `LoginScreen` that represents the login screen of an Android app built using Jetpack Compose. It handles user input, login state, and navigation to the home screen. The file also includes a `GoogleSignInButton` Composable function and a `performGoogleSignIn` function that handles Google Sign-In functionality.\\n\\n`LoginScreen`: Composable function that renders the login screen with email, password, and Google Sign-In options.\\n\\n`GoogleSignInButton`: Composable function that renders a button for Google Sign-In.\\n\\n`performGoogleSignIn`: Function that handles the Google Sign-In process using the Google Identity SDK.\\n\\n`submitLogin`: Function that validates the email and password input and triggers the login process.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/RegisterScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function `RegisterScreen` that represents the registration screen of an Android app built using Jetpack Compose. The file also includes a helper function `submitRegister` that handles the validation of the user\\u0027s input during the registration process.\\n\\n`RegisterScreen`: Displays the registration screen with input fields for name, email, password, and confirm password, and handles the registration process using the provided `AuthViewModel`.\\n\\n`submitRegister`: Validates the user\\u0027s input, including checking for empty fields, valid email format, and password matching, and calls the provided callback functions with the validation results.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/MainScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a Composable function `MainBottomBar` that renders a Material3 `NavigationBar` with four tabs: Home, Meditate, Stats, and Profile. The file also defines a `BottomTab` data class and a list of `TABS` that represent the bottom navigation bar items.\\n\\n`BottomTab`: A data class that represents a bottom navigation bar item with a route, label, and icon.\\n\\n`MainBottomBar`: A Composable function that renders a Material3 `NavigationBar` with the bottom navigation bar items defined in the `TABS` list. The function takes a `NavController` and the current route as parameters, and updates the selected tab based on the current route.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/MeditationRegularityScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`MeditationRegularityScreen`: This function displays a screen that shows the user\\u0027s meditation statistics, including total meditation time, best streak, and sessions this week.\\n\\nThe file also imports various Compose UI components and a ViewModel class (`StatsViewModel`) to retrieve and display the user\\u0027s meditation data.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/BreatheViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a ViewModel class for a breathing exercise app, along with several supporting data classes and enums. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions:\\n\\n`BreathPhase`: An enum representing the different phases of the breathing exercise.\\n\\n`SessionFeedback`: A data class representing the feedback data collected after a meditation session.\\n\\n`BreatheState`: A data class representing the current state of the breathing exercise.\\n\\n`BreatheViewModel`: The main ViewModel class that manages the state of the breathing exercise, including starting and stopping the exercise, handling elapsed time, and submitting feedback.\\n\\n`startExercise()`: Starts the breathing exercise by running the different phases in a loop.\\n\\n`runPhase(BreathPhase, Int)`: Runs a single phase of the breathing exercise, updating the state accordingly.\\n\\n`startElapsedTimer()`: Starts a timer that updates the elapsed time of the exercise.\\n\\n`endSession()`: Stops the exercise and prepares to show the feedback modal.\\n\\n`submitFeedback(SessionFeedback)`: Submits the user\\u0027s feedback to the API and saves the session locally.\\n\\n`skipFeedback()`: Saves the session with default feedback values.\",\"/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a top-level build file that sets up common configuration options for all sub-projects or modules in an Android application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but instead, it defines the following plugins:\\n\\n`com.android.application`: Applies the Android application plugin.\\n`org.jetbrains.kotlin.android`: Applies the Kotlin Android plugin.\\n`org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.compose`: Applies the Kotlin Compose plugin.\\n`com.google.dagger.hilt.android`: Applies the Dagger Hilt Android plugin.\\n`com.google.devtools.ksp`: Applies the Kotlin Symbol Processing (KSP) plugin.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to centralize the version numbers and apply these plugins to all sub-projects or modules in the Android application, ensuring consistency across the entire project.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/viewmodel/ChallengesViewModel.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `ChallengesViewModel`, which is a ViewModel class responsible for managing the state and logic related to challenges in a mobile application.\\n\\n`ChallengesViewModel`: A ViewModel class that manages the state and logic related to challenges, including available challenges, user\\u0027s challenges, challenge recommendations, and various challenge-related operations.\\n\\n`loadAvailableChallenges()`: Loads the available challenges from the repository and updates the corresponding state.\\n\\n`loadMyChallenges()`: Loads the user\\u0027s challenges from the repository and updates the corresponding state.\\n\\n`loadRecommendation()`: Loads the challenge recommendation from the repository and updates the corresponding state.\\n\\n`joinChallenge(slug: String)`: Joins a challenge with the given slug and updates the user\\u0027s challenges.\\n\\n`checkIn(challengeId: String)`: Checks in to a challenge with the given ID and updates the user\\u0027s challenges.\\n\\n`abandonChallenge(challengeId: String)`: Abandons a challenge with the given ID and updates the user\\u0027s challenges.\\n\\n`init`: Initializes the ViewModel by loading the available challenges, user\\u0027s challenges, and challenge recommendation.\",\"/app/src/test/java/com/example/breathe/ExampleUnitTest.kt\":\"This file contains a single unit test class with one test function. The file does not define any classes or functions, but rather contains a single unit test that checks if the addition of 2 and 2 is correct.\\n\\n`ExampleUnitTest`: A unit test class that contains a single test function to verify the correctness of a simple addition operation.\\n\\n`addition_isCorrect()`: A test function that checks if the addition of 2 and 2 is equal to 4, which is the expected result.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/ui/screens/NavGraph.kt\":\"This file defines the navigation graph for the Breathe app, which is the main entry point for the app\\u0027s UI. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`Route`: A class that defines the various routes (screens) in the app, including splash, login, register, home, breathe, meditation, profile, history, meditation regularity, and session history.\\n\\n`AppNavGraph`: A function that sets up the single-activity navigation graph for the app, handling back navigation and managing the visibility of the bottom bar based on the current route.\\n\\n`BOTTOM_BAR_ROUTES`: A private set of routes where the bottom bar should be visible.\\n\\nThe file also includes the implementation of the various screens (composables) that are part of the navigation graph, such as `SplashScreen`, `LoginScreen`, `RegisterScreen`, `HomeScreen`, `BreatheScreen`, `ProfileScreen`, `StatsScreen`, `MeditationRegularityScreen`, and `HistoryScreen`.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/BreatheApp.kt\":\"This file defines the main application class for the \\\"Breathe\\\" Android app, which is annotated with `@HiltAndroidApp` to enable Dagger Hilt dependency injection.\\n\\n`BreatheApp`: The main application class that initializes the Room database, sets up the WorkManager configuration, and schedules a worker to sync any failed sessions.\\n\\n`workManagerConfiguration`: A property that configures the WorkManager to use the Hilt-generated worker factory, allowing for dependency injection in the workers.\\n\\n`onCreate()`: Initializes the Room database on the first launch and schedules a worker to sync any failed sessions.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/breathe/utils/NotificationScheduler.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`NotificationScheduler`: A class that schedules and cancels daily breathing reminders using the `AlarmManager` and `NotificationManager` APIs.\\n\\n`ensureChannelExists()`: Ensures that the notification channel for the daily breathing reminders exists.\\n\\n`schedule(hour: Int, minute: Int)`: Schedules (or reschedules) the daily alarm for the specified time, ensuring that the alarm fires tomorrow if the time has already passed today.\\n\\n`cancel()`: Cancels the scheduled daily alarm.\\n\\n`canScheduleExact()`: Checks if the device can schedule exact alarms, which is required on Android 12 (API level 31) and higher.\\n\\n`nextTriggerMillis(hour: Int, minute: Int)`: Calculates the next trigger time for the daily alarm based on the specified hour and minute.\\n\\n`alarmIntent()`: Creates a `PendingIntent` for the `ReminderReceiver` class, which is used to trigger the daily alarm.\"}"}
